title: Classifying an Angle by Its Measure

steps.instruction: What are the angle types and their ranges?
steps.work: Acute: 0° to 90° (not including 90°). Right: exactly 90°. Obtuse: 90° to 180°. Straight: exactly 180°. Reflex: 180° to 360°.
steps.explanation: Each type covers a specific range of degree measurements.

steps.work: 135° is greater than 90° and less than 180°.
steps.explanation: We check which range 135 falls into.
Any angle between 90° and 180° (not including the endpoints) is obtuse.

title: Identifying Angle Types on a Clock

steps.instruction: How many degrees between each hour on a clock?
steps.explanation: The clock face is a full circle (360°) divided into 12 equal sections.

steps.instruction: What angle do the hands form at 3 o'clock?
steps.work: From 12 to 3 is 3 hours. 3 × 30° = 90°. This is a right angle.
steps.explanation: The hands are 3 hour-marks apart, each worth 30°.

steps.instruction: What angle do the hands form at 5 o'clock?
steps.work: From 12 to 5 is 5 hours. 5 × 30° = 150°. This is an obtuse angle.
steps.explanation: 150° is between 90° and 180°, so it is obtuse.

steps.instruction: What angle do the hands form at 2 o'clock?
steps.work: From 12 to 2 is 2 hours. 2 × 30° = 60°. This is an acute angle.
steps.explanation: 60° is less than 90°, so it is acute.
